runs.topic: Confirmation Bias Hijacks Your Brain
runs.title: Confirmation Bias Hijacks Your Brain
runs.description: Explain confirmation bias with a concrete example. Ground in cognitive psychology. No partisan framing.

runs.topic: Your Body Clock Runs on Light
runs.title: Your Body Clock Runs on Light
runs.description: Explain circadian rhythms and SCN light entrainment. Ground in chronobiology. Avoid extreme biohacking claims.

runs.topic: Dopamine Is Not the Pleasure Molecule
runs.title: Dopamine Is Not the Pleasure Molecule
runs.description: Explain dopamine as motivation/reward prediction error, not pure pleasure. Ground in neuroscience consensus; avoid biohacker overclaims.

runs.topic: How We Find Planets We Can't See
runs.title: How We Find Planets We Can't See

runs.title: What Causes the Northern Lights

runs.title: GPS Needs Einstein to Work

runs.title: How a Star's Death Makes Your Atoms

runs.title: Mycelium: The Living Network Under Forests
runs.description: Explain mycelium and mycorrhizal networks. Avoid overstating 'wood wide web' communication claims; present evidence and debate fairly.

runs.title: What the Habitable Zone Really Means
runs.description: Explain circumstellar habitable zone carefully. Cite NASA exoplanet education. No alien life claims.

runs.title: Why Mitochondria Are the Powerhouses

runs.title: Neural Nets Are Not Digital Brains

runs.title: How Your Immune System Remembers

runs.title: The Carbon Cycle in 60 Seconds

runs.title: Neutrinos Pass Through You Every Second

runs.title: What Herd Immunity Actually Means

runs.title: Why Humpback Whales Sing

runs.title: Bacteria Build Cities Called Biofilms

runs.title: What Stem Cells Can and Can't Do

runs.title: Telomeres: The Caps on Your Chromosomes

runs.title: CRISPR Crops Are Not the Same as GMOs of the 90s
